senses.definition: the fact of being aware of information that is known to few people
examples: he is always in the know

senses.definition: be cognizant or aware of a fact or a specific piece of information; possess knowledge or information about
examples: I know that the President lied to the people
I want to know who is winning the game!
I know it's time

senses.definition: know how to do or perform something
examples: She knows how to knit
Does your husband know how to cook?

senses.definition: be familiar or acquainted with a person or an object
examples: She doesn't know this composer
Do you know my sister?
We know this movie
I know him under a different name
This flower is known as a Peruvian Lily

senses.definition: have firsthand knowledge of states, situations, emotions, or sensations
examples: I know the feeling!
have you ever known hunger?
I have lived a kind of hell when I was a drug addict
The holocaust survivors have lived a nightmare
I lived through two divorces

senses.definition: accept (someone) to be what is claimed or accept their power and authority
examples: The Crown Prince was acknowledged as the true heir to the throne
We do not recognize your gods
I know Latin
This student knows her irregular verbs
Do you know the poem well enough to recite it?
Adam knew Eve
Were you ever intimate with this man?

senses.definition: know the nature or character of
examples: we all knew her as a big show-off

senses.definition: be able to distinguish, recognize as being different
examples: The child knows right from wrong
I know this voice!
